title: Operationalize 'mistakes of Rust' in the persona
description: Lines 14-17 say the reviewer 'watched Rust make some deeply unfortunate choices' and 'does not want to repeat them' — but never says what those choices are. The reviewer is given an attitude but not a checklist. The whole point of this persona is to apply a specific DX lens; leaving the anti-patterns implicit defeats that. Fix: add a concrete list of Rust DX failure modes the reviewer should actively watch for: lifetime annotation burden, async coloring problem, trait object vs generic explosion, macro system escaping type safety, borrow-checker fighting that forces unsafe, error type proliferation, coherence/orphan rules preventing extensibility.
close_reason: All four PROMPT-review-lang.md fixes applied in one pass.
